quote_of_day.text:
>>26687
>is that what peak of loneliness
I was abandoned by my own mother, I don't want to be dramatic but you can't argue against it.
it is not even that bad, sometimes I feel a lot freer than other people.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of self-improvement swirled over the board, as one anon claimed a transformation into a 'pretty boy' after weight loss and a clean shave. This drew a scattered response but little sustained attention.

A high-pressure front of caste claims and ethnic divisions dominated the discussion, with one anon welcoming only certain groups to their thread. This sparked a sharp exchange, with participants trading insults and claims.

Meanwhile, a fog of uncertainty rolled in around Indiachan's rating, with one anon seeking a simple 'rate' from others. A cold snap of intimacy concerns also chilled the board, as another anon shared their worries about their parents' sex life.

A light drizzle of nostalgia touched down when an anon asked about old Indiachan users and their lore.

articles.title: Anon Claims Weight Loss and Clean Shave Reveal 'Pretty Boy Features'
articles.body:
Anon posted about recently losing weight and shaving, claiming to have developed 'pretty boy features.' They expressed a desire to 'leanmaxx' further, believing this will make women who rejected them in school regret their decision.

The original poster identified having a 'slightly wide oval face' as the only issue preventing them from becoming a 'handsome pretty boy.' Another anon responded by suggesting that 'women love a man that can defend her,' implying that physical strength, not just appearance, is attractive.

This poster recommended 'bulkmaxxing' over 'leanmaxxing.' A different anon shared their own experience of gaining weight from 69 to 78 kilograms. One respondent warned about the downsides of 'leaning,' stating it can lead to hair loss and decreased bone density.

Another anon mocked the original poster's goal, calling them a 'protein deficient bimaru chamar' and implying they are not familiar with proper nutrition. Anon also jokingly suggested the original poster should share a picture of their penis with foreskin or their body.

A brief discussion followed about the original poster being asked to post pictures, with one request being to 'post body.' The conversation revolved around physical appearance, attractiveness, and methods to improve one's looks or physique.

The discussion included personal experiences, advice, and mocking comments about the original poster's goals and appearance.

articles.title: Caste Claims and Ethnic Divisions on Indiachan
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread claiming that only certain ethnic groups, such as Kashmiri Pandits, Hariyanvi, Punjabi, Jaats, Marathi, Pahadis, Bengalis, and Dravidians, are welcomed.

One respondent asked if Pahadis are considered tribals, and another anon replied that they are according to the board's consensus.

A Himachali Brahmin asked if they are considered upper castes, and an anon replied that they are in, with the northern command.

Anon discussed the classification of certain castes and ethnicities, stating that many are called SC/ST due to land laws and to protect their land and culture from Bimarus.

Another claimed that caste is based on occupation and that individuals have nothing in common with their ancestors' castes.

The thread also saw claims of ethnic superiority and abuse, with one anon stating that people often mistake them for Uttrakhandi khasiyas and abuse them, lumping them with them despite their different culture.

Anon also joked about Bimaru groping a foreigner and making news in Western media. The discussion also touched on the topic of upper castes, with one anon listing Iyers, Namboothiris, Reddys, and Nairs as examples.

Another anon questioned whether Dravidians are now considered upper castes, asking if there is a caste expert on the board. Throughout the thread, various slurs and insults were hurled, with one anon telling another to 'fuck off'.

The conversation was marked by a tone of hostility and division, with various anons making claims and counter-claims about caste and ethnicity.

articles.title: Anon seeks rating for Indiachan
articles.body:
Anon started a thread asking for a rating, phrasing it as 'Bad or worse?' One respondent described the site as having 'Great moderation shit userbase', drawing a comparison with another site they characterised as having 'shit moderation great userbase'.

Another anon expressed dissatisfaction, citing too many users they referred to with derogatory terms, but noted the moderators and administrators were good, giving it a 2/10 rating.

A different poster mentioned missing a user named Dyaush, who they thought was a bad administrator but a good poster. Some users defended certain aspects of the site.

One anon preferred it over another site they associated with a 'bhangi admin and censorisation'. A different user thought it was better than another option. However, one anon described the site as 'Dead'. The ratings varied widely.

One user mentioned needing 'good UC posters' to boost the rating to 6/10. Another anon simply stated it was 'so bad, cant even rate'. A third anon rated it positively, saying 'good'.

articles.title: Anon asks about old Indiachan users
articles.body:
Anon posted about old Indiachans and their lore. The original poster asked if any old users, referred to as "oldchhakka," were still present and to share their experiences. One respondent described themselves as a "rusty old fag" and advised moving on from nostalgia.

Another user reminisced about the early Indiachans, recalling their intolerance for Muslims. A question was raised about the whereabouts of "Aditya Dan." One user claimed to have been in Aditya Dan's server. There were also mentions of a "gayming server" active during lockdown.

articles.title: Anon shares family intimacy concerns
articles.body:
Anon asked if their parents are still sexually active, sharing they have become a shut-in and don't know their parents' age or intimacy status.

One respondent shared their parents verbally abuse each other, and one is likely asexual. Another anon mentioned discovering their parents using affectionate emojis in a chat.

Other anons shared their own parents' lack of intimacy, including one who said their parents had a "dead bedroom" since their birth.
